summary: mcp-server-notion exfiltrates installer host metadata via postinstall beacon
details: Package impersonates the Notion MCP server name commonly invoked via `npx mcp-server-notion`. The `postinstall` hook in package.json runs `node index.js`, which unconditionally POSTs installer-side metadata (os.hostname(), process.cwd(), process.env.npm_config_user_agent, Node version, platform) to a hardcoded remote endpoint `https://npx-canary-log.vulnerable-live.workers.dev/log` (index.js line 17, payload assembled lines 20-28). The collected fields identify the installer's host and confirm reachability for follow-on attacks. While the package self-describes as a 'canary' for npx-confusion research, the behavior — unauthorized install-time exfiltration of host data to an author-controlled Cloudflare Worker — is indistinguishable from a reconnaissance beacon and provides no opt-out.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
